result:
## Summary

The issue requested deep link support for the **Subscribers tab** and **Traffic tab with appropriate granularity** in the Stats section.

### Root Cause

The `StatsLinkHandler.toStatsTimeframe()` method in `StatsLinkHandler.kt:94-103` only mapped 5 URL path values (`day`, `week`, `month`, `year`, `insights`) to `StatsTimeframe` enum values. It was missing mappings for `"subscribers"` and `"traffic"`, even though the `StatsTimeframe` enum already had `SUBSCRIBERS` and `TRAFFIC` values and the downstream handling (`StatsViewModel.getInitialTimeFrame()`) already correctly mapped them to `StatsSection.SUBSCRIBERS` and `StatsSection.TRAFFIC`.

Additionally, the `buildNavigateAction()` method had a parsing limitation: when a URL like `https://wordpress.com/stats/subscribers` (without a site) was provided, the last path segment was only checked against `toSite()` (which returned null for "subscribers"), and the second-to-last segment was "stats" (not a valid timeframe), so it fell through to `OpenStats` instead of `OpenStatsForTimeframe(SUBSCRIBERS)`.

### Changes Made

**Single file modified:** `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/deeplinks/handlers/StatsLinkHandler.kt`

1. **Added `"subscribers"` and `"traffic"` mappings** in `toStatsTimeframe()` (line 105-106)
2. **Added `lastSegmentTimeframe` fallback** in `buildNavigateAction()` (line 36, 48-50) — when the last segment isn't recognized as a site and the second-to-last isn't a valid timeframe, the last segment is also checked as a potential timeframe

### Supported Deep Links After Fix

| URL | Result |
|---|---|
| `https://wordpress.com/stats/subscribers/example.com` | Opens Stats → Subscribers tab for site |
| `https://wordpress.com/stats/subscribers` | Opens Stats → Subscribers tab for current site |
| `https://wordpress.com/stats/traffic/example.com` | Opens Stats → Traffic tab for site |
| `https://wordpress.com/stats/traffic` | Opens Stats → Traffic tab for current site |
| `https://wordpress.com/stats/day/example.com` | Opens Stats → Traffic tab with Day granularity (existing, works via `setupDeeplinkForTrafficTab`) |
| `https://wordpress.com/stats/day` | Opens Stats → Traffic tab with Day granularity for current site (was broken, now fixed) |
